seo.title: Iowa Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Iowa — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license iowa, dental assistant certification iowa, RDA iowa, DANB CDA iowa, dental assistant radiography iowa

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Iowa.

steps.title: Meet Education Requirements
steps.description: Complete a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or meet the requirements for on-the-job training as specified by the Iowa Dental Board.

steps.title: Complete Application
steps.description: Submit a complete Dental Assistant Registration application to the Iowa Dental Board.

steps.title: Receive Registration
steps.description: Upon approval of your application, receive your Iowa Dental Assistant Registration.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the Iowa Dental Board by registration.

faqs.question: Does Iowa require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Iowa requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Iowa?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Iowa.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Iowa?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Iowa?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $40. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the Iowa Dental Board by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Iowa
hero.dek: Iowa Dental Assistant registration requires graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or completion of specific on-the-job training, and application to the Iowa Dental Board.
